issues.summary: [Microsite UCS] Verify bucket and Kafka topic alignment
issues.description:
### Description

Confirm infrastructure alignment between DSP and UCS. No code changes — just verification and documentation.

### Acceptance Criteria

* Confirm: `rtb-microsite-assets-production` = `micrositeOriginalCreativeBucket` in UCS config (check `app-config` in unified-creative-service)
* Confirm: `sdsp.ucs.stream` is the topic UCS workflow-service publishes to for tenant `trader` (check Kafka config)
* Confirm: `adType: "microsite_video"` is preserved as-is in Kafka callback (trace through workflow-service code)
* Document findings in integration-plan

issues.summary: [Microsite UCS] Timeout detection for stuck transcoding jobs
issues.description:
### Description

Scheduled job to detect MicrositeVideos stuck in `:transcoding` status for >30 minutes and mark them as `:failed`.

### Acceptance Criteria

* `Microsites::UseCases::MarkStuckTranscodingFailed` use case
* Transitions stuck videos to `:failed`
* Updates parent microsite to `:partially_failed`
* Scheduled via cron (every 15 minutes)
* Unit tests

### User Story

As a backend developer,  
I want a background job that generates a self-contained HTML page from microsite data and uploads it to S3,  
so that Samsung TVs can render the microsite via CloudFront.

### Context

After all video assets are transcoded (UCS integration, Phase 2), the system generates a static HTML file containing: background image (4K), video players with variant selection (JS picks 1080p or 720p based on TV capabilities), app deeplinks, and VAST-style tracker firing (quartile events, impression, exit). The HTML is uploaded to S3 at `microsite/{id}/index.html` and served via CloudFront.

**BLOCKED by UCS integration** — cannot generate final HTML until transcoded video variants are available.

### Business Value

The final deliverable of the microsites pipeline. Without HTML generation, there is nothing to serve on Samsung TVs — the entire feature is incomplete.

### Acceptance Criteria

* \[ \] `Microsites::UseCases::GenerateHtml` (Ritual): build_payload → render_template → upload_to_s3 → update_microsite
* \[ \] `Microsites::Tasks::GenerateHtml` (Tasks::Base): calls use case, handles failure
* \[ \] ERB template generates valid, self-contained HTML
* \[ \] JS in template: video player with quartile tracking (start, firstQuartile, midpoint, thirdQuartile, complete)
* \[ \] JS resolution detection: picks video variant based on TV capabilities
* \[ \] Background image: always 4K, TV scales down
* \[ \] S3 upload path: `microsite/{microsite.id}/index.html`
* \[ \] Payload snapshot saved to `microsites.configuration` JSONB
* \[ \] microsite.processing_status transitions to `ready` on success, `failed` on error
* \[ \] Retry: 3 attempts
* \[ \] Unit tests for use case (mocked S3, template rendering tested independently)

### Technical Details

**Files:**

* `packs/microsites/app/lib/microsites/use_cases/generate_html.rb`
* `packs/microsites/app/services/microsites/tasks/generate_html.rb`
* `packs/microsites/app/views/microsites/templates/mic

issues.summary: [Microsite P2] General Settings Section UI Fixes
issues.description:
## CONTEXT

The General Settings section UI has diverged from the updated designs. Several layout, structure, and content changes are needed to align the current implementation with the new mockups.

**Current state (broken):** Fields include Name, Advertiser, Country, 3rd Party Impression Tracker, Exit Tracker — all in one "General Settings" collapsible section. Layout uses left-aligned labels with inconsistent spacing. Section wrapping is incorrect.

**Target state (new design):** General Settings contains only Microsite Name and Advertiser. Background and Features are separate collapsible sections. Layout is cleaner with proper field alignment and updated placeholders.

## CHANGES REQUIRED

### 1. Restructure Sections

| Current | Target |
| --- | --- |
| General Settings (Name, Advertiser, Country, 3rd Party Impression Tracker, Exit Tracker)  | General Settings (Microsite Name, Advertiser only)  |
| Main Content (Background Image, Video, Click to App)                                      | Background (Background Image, Impression Tracker)   |
| —                                                                                         | Features (Video toggle, Click to App toggle)        |

* **Remove** Country field from General Settings
* **Remove** 3rd Party Impression Tracker and Exit Tracker from General Settings
* **Move** Impression Tracker to new "Background" section
* **Create** new "Background" collapsible section with: Background Image uploader + Impression Tracker
* **Create** new "Features" collapsible section with: Video toggle + Click to App toggle
* Each section should be independently collapsible with chevron icon

### 2. Field Label & Placeholder Updates

| Field | Old Label | New Label | New Placeholder |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name                | Name                            | Microsite Name                  | _(empty)_                       |
| Advertiser          | Advertiser                      | Advertiser                      |

issues.summary: [Microsite P2] Implement rtb-delivery endpoint and handler
issues.description:
## BUSINESS IMPACT

Microsite pages on Samsung TVs need to report user interactions beyond basic impressions. Without support for `video_start`, `app_click`, and `exit` (with `time_spent`) tracker values, Campaign Managers have no visibility into video engagement, CTA effectiveness, or session duration — making it impossible to optimize creative strategy or justify microsite ad spend.

## USER STORY

As a Campaign Manager, I want microsite interactions (video start, app click, exit + time spent) to be tracked and reported in the DSP, so that I can measure video engagement, CTA performance, and session duration to optimize campaign creative strategy.

## CONTEXT

**What we know from the PRD:**

* Microsite must collect: impressions, video start, video quartiles (25/50/75/100%), app click (CTA), exit + time spent
* Data must be visible in the DSP Reporting Dashboard (for Campaign Managers)
* 3P pixels (impression tracker, exit tracker) — we already have input fields in the DSP UI

**What already exists:**

* The `/microsite/:info/:filename` endpoint pattern is defined in [task-microsite-delivery-tracker-endpoint.md](https://jadujoel.github.io/markdown-to-jira/task-microsite-delivery-tracker-endpoint.md)
* rtb-delivery has existing endpoints `/impressions/`, `/trackers/`, `/clicks/`
* Avro schema has `misc` field (map<string,string>) — can carry `time_spent`
* DSP UI has `impressionTracker` and `exitTracker` fields in `main-content-section.tsx`

## SCOPE

Implement the handler and request module for the `/microsite/:info/:filename` endpoint in rtb-delivery that processes the following new tracker values:

1. `video_start` — fired when user initiates video playback
2. `app_click` — fired when user clicks the CTA button (app install/open)
3. `exit` — fired when user leaves the microsite, with `time_spent` as a query parameter (seconds)
4. `impression`
5. All other video trackers

The endpoint must:

1. Decode the INFO blob (bid context)
2. Set correct event and tracker f

## BUSINESS IMPACT

The Microsite section currently lives inside the DSP shell (`rtb-trader/ui/dsp/src/pages/microsites/`). As the feature grows in complexity (form builder, preview panel, listing, backend integration, asset uploads), keeping it in the DSP shell creates deployment coupling — any microsite change requires a full DSP shell deploy, and vice versa. Extracting microsites into their own micro frontend (MFE) enables independent deployments, faster CI/CD, smaller bundle sizes, and team autonomy — matching the established pattern used by `creatives-management`, `planning`, `sellers-ui`, `supply`, and `targeting` realms.

## USER STORY

As a developer working on the Microsite feature, I want the microsite UI to be a standalone micro frontend with its own build, deploy, and hosting infrastructure, so that I can develop and deploy microsite changes independently without coupling to the DSP shell release cycle.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

* New workspace `rtb-trader/ui/microsites-management/` created in the monorepo
* Module Federation configured — exposes `remoteEntry.js` with microsite routes
* DSP shell (`rtb-trader/ui/dsp`) consumes the new MFE as a remote
* All needed pages/components migrated from `rtb-trader/ui/dsp/src/pages/microsites/` to the new workspace
* Independent build and dev server works (`pnpm dev` in the new workspace)
* GHA workflow for deploying to S3 (staging + prod)
* Terraform configuration in `module-federation-ui-tf` for the new realm (S3 + CloudFront + WAF)
